rulings.Case Title: WINSTON S NICHOLS ET AL VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The Claims For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 29, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Parker-Hannifin Corp.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is continued to April 24, 2018 pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. section 437c(h) request for a continuance, which is granted. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by April 16, 2018. Supplemental reply, if any, due by April 19, 2018. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m., Hon. Lynn O'Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/LOT)
